task.title: Reject stored payload version zero explicitly during replay
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/382 by preserving the approved one-based payload-version contract when corrupted or externally modified SQLite rows contain payload_version zero.
acceptance_criteria: Replaying a row whose payload_version is zero returns ReplayError::InvalidStoredPayloadVersion(0) with a stable diagnostic.
The invalid stored payload-version error has no underlying source and is returned before typed decoding.
Supported positive and out-of-range stored payload versions retain their existing classifications.
The public documentation describes stored payload versions below one as invalid range failures.
The pinned local and pull-request quality gates pass.

attempts.summary: Added a focused integration test that bypasses SQLite CHECK constraints to persist payload version zero.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test panicked in DecoderMustNotRun::decode, proving replay accepted representable zero and dispatched it to extensible typed decoding instead of rejecting the out-of-domain stored value.

attempts.summary: Added an explicit lower-bound guard at the replay conversion boundary.
attempts.patch: After checked i64-to-u32 conversion, replay now maps zero to ReplayError::InvalidStoredPayloadVersion before reading payload bytes or invoking Event::decode.

attempts.summary: Verified stable diagnostics and preserved neighboring replay behavior.
attempts.patch: The regression test asserts the exact variant, display text, absent source, and decoder bypass; focused tests for supported unknown and out-of-range payload versions remained green.

attempts.summary: Completed independent review and monitored the required pull-request checks.
attempts.diagnostic: Independent read-only pre-commit review found no blocking correctness issues. Rust quality gate run 29310419413 passed, and CodeRabbit completed with no actionable comments; pull request 383 squash-merged as fa80399.

outcome.summary: Replay now classifies stored payload version zero as an explicit invalid persisted value and rejects it before typed decoder dispatch while preserving positive compatibility semantics.

verification.command: Independent read-only pre-commit review and TDD evidence: https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/384#issuecomment-4965937780
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29310419413 for pull request 383
CodeRabbit review for pull request 383: review completed with no actionable comments and required status passed
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0015-zero-stored-payload-version.json # result: valid development record
/run/current-system/sw/bin/nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 15 valid, 0 invalid

lessons: Validate persisted values against semantic domain bounds after representational conversion; fitting in u32 does not make zero a valid payload version.
Use a panic-on-call decoder to prove replay rejects corrupted metadata before crossing into extensible user code.
Preserve positive unsupported-version handling while separating non-positive persisted metadata into InvalidStoredPayloadVersion.
Bypassing database CHECK constraints in a RED test makes defensive replay behavior observable without weakening the production schema.
